title: Error: unable to connect to Podman socket illegal base64 data at input byte 372
body:
I am trying to follow the Podman Windows tutorial, but when I run the "podman run ubi8-micro date" I get the error message: "Cannot connect to Podman. Please verify your connection to the Linux system using `podman system connection list`, or try `podman machine init` and `podman machine start` to manage a new Linux VM
Error: unable to connect to Podman socket: knownhosts: C:\Users\thoma\.ssh\known_hosts:3: illegal base64 data at input byte 372"

I have tried stopping and restarting the podman machine, but that did not resolve my issue. When I run the "podman system connection list" command, it shows the "podman-machine-default" and "podman-machine-default-root" machines.

I am brand new to podman and containers in general so any advice would be greatly appreciated.

comments.body:
I'm seeing a similar problem on macos:

```
starksm@Scotts-Mac-Studio /tmp % podman info
OS: darwin/arm64
provider: applehv
version: 5.3.1

Cannot connect to Podman. Please verify your connection to the Linux system using `podman system connection list`, or try `podman machine init` and `podman machine start` to manage a new Linux VM
Error: unable to connect to Podman socket: knownhosts: /Users/starksm/.ssh/known_hosts:5: illegal base64 data at input byte 7
starksm@Scotts-Mac-Studio /tmp % sw_vers
ProductName:		macOS
ProductVersion:		15.1.1
BuildVersion:		24B91
```

A podman machine was created and started from the podman desktop, Version 1.15.0

I can run a container and connect to the running machine instance from within the desktop, but not a terminal window.

Am having the exact same issue.

```
% podman info
OS: darwin/amd64
provider: applehv
version: 5.3.1

Cannot connect to Podman. Please verify your connection to the Linux system using `podman system connection list`, or try `podman machine init` and `podman machine start` to manage a new Linux VM
Error: unable to connect to Podman socket: knownhosts: /Users/yuhang/.ssh/known_hosts:25: illegal base64 data at input byte 372
```
Same here, using the dmg installer, and following the onboarding, i ended up having the same issue.
Did you check what byte is in that file at the reported location?

I had the same issue. I went through the known_hosts file and found a wrong entry. 

I had a missing new line character due to which the 6th line was merged into the 5th line. After I separated the same into 2 lines, the issue was resolved and the podman commands started working with no issues.

The following image shows the command before and after I changed the wrong entry in known_hosts
![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/b218b31e-1643-4d78-948b-a653d4dad2b3)

FAULTY KNOWN_HOSTS
![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/c61a6914-cefa-4c46-bb8a-d08bea5ba4d5)

FIXED KNOWN_HOSTS
![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/1708a784-0304-4476-b062-8370b9865cf5)

So after 3 weeks of trying to figure out what was wrong, I looked in my known_hosts file at the specified byte index, but found nothing out of the ordinary. I then decided to just delete both my known_hosts and my known_hosts_OLD files, which then fixed my issue.

I think the solution is to pay attention to what the error message says:

`Error: unable to connect to Podman socket: knownhosts: /Users/etyrer/.ssh/known_hosts:**556**: illegal base64 data at input byte 3`

the problem is line 556

so I did vi  ~/.ssh/known_hosts and looked at line 556 and it was mangled so I deleted that line and then ran:

```
➜ podman run quay.io/podman/hello
Trying to pull quay.io/podman/hello:latest...

 ~/  o  o \~~~~.----. ~~
```
